The **Torneo DIMAYOR**, referred to as **Torneo BetPlay Dimayor** due to [sponsorship](/wiki/Sponsor_(commercial) "Sponsor (commercial)") by online betting company [BetPlay](/w/index.php?title=BetPlay&action=edit&redlink=1 "BetPlay (page does not exist)"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) is the second-division [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in Colombia. The tournament was inaugurated on 29 January 1991.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

[DIMAYOR](/wiki/DIMAYOR "DIMAYOR") has applied a new format from 2015 onwards because of the expansion to 20 teams in the Primera A tournament. The league will no longer have Apertura and Finalización seasons but rather a yearlong tournament with home and away games. The first phase of the tournament is everyone against everyone home and away games. The second is a two-groups phase with the 8 top teams from the season who play each other to determine the winners of each group. The winners of the two groups are both promoted to the Primera A and play the final in home and away games to determine the champion of the season.

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-fn1_4-0)** Due to the impact of the [COVID-19 pandemic in Colombia](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Colombia "COVID-19 pandemic in Colombia"), DIMAYOR decided to suspend promotion and relegation in the 2020 season. As a result of this situation, it was agreed that the 2021–I tournament champion would face the 2020 Primera B tournament champion in a double-legged series (*Grand Final*) to decide one promoted team, which was also considered a tournament champion. The 2021–I tournament featured two champions: Deportes Quindío as 2021–I tournament champion and Atlético Huila as Grand Final 2021–I champion.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-fn2_6-0)** Champion of the 2021–I Categoría Primera B.[[4]](#cite_note-5)
3. **[^](#cite_ref-fn3_8-0)** Champion of the 2021–I Categoría Primera B Grand Final.[[5]](#cite_note-7)
4. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-2025_season_11-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-2025_season_11-1) The regulations for the 2025 season originally considered a final series between the winners of the season's short tournaments.[[6]](#cite_note-9) However, since the Torneo I winners Jaguares also placed as one of the top two teams in the aggregate table, they were automatically promoted and the grand final to decide a single season champion was not held.[[7]](#cite_note-10)
